abseqr 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Analysing Nanobody CDR3 Sequences with abseqr

Read a Chothia-renumbered PDB file into a tidy tibble

Source: R/chothia_pdb.R
read_chothia_pdb.Rd

Parses ATOM records from a SAbDab Chothia-renumbered PDB file into a tidy tibble. Each row represents one atom. Residue numbers with insertion codes (e.g. 100A, 82B) are preserved as character strings, which is essential for correct Chothia CDR boundary handling.

read_chothia_pdb(path, atoms = "CA")

Arguments

path

Path to a Chothia-renumbered PDB file.

atoms

Character vector of atom names to retain. Default "CA" (alpha carbons only). Pass NULL to keep all atoms.

Value

A tibble with columns:

record

Character. Record type: "ATOM" or "HETATM".

atom_serial

Integer. Atom serial number.

atom_name

Character. Atom name (e.g. "CA").

res_name

Character. Three-letter residue name.

chain_id

Character. Chain identifier.

res_seq

Character. Residue sequence number, including any insertion code (e.g. "100A").

res_num

Integer. Numeric part of residue sequence number.

ins_code

Character. Insertion code, or "" if none.

x, y, z

Numeric. Cartesian coordinates in Angstroms.

aa1

Character. One-letter amino acid code, or NA for non-standard residues.

chain_type

Character. "H" (heavy), "L" (light), or "other" — inferred from REMARK 5 lines.

Details

Chain type (heavy/light/other) is inferred from the SAbDab REMARK 5 header lines when present.

Examples

pdb_file <- system.file("extdata", "1f2x.pdb", package = "abseqr")
atoms <- read_chothia_pdb(pdb_file)
atoms
#> # A tibble: 252 × 13
#>    record atom_serial atom_name res_name chain_id res_seq res_num ins_code     x
#>    <chr>        <int> <chr>     <chr>    <chr>    <chr>     <int> <chr>    <dbl>
#>  1 ATOM             2 CA        GLN      K        1             1 ""        7.79
#>  2 ATOM             7 CA        VAL      K        2             2 ""        6.29
#>  3 ATOM            14 CA        GLN      K        3             3 ""        8.58
#>  4 ATOM            23 CA        LEU      K        4             4 ""        7.99
#>  5 ATOM            31 CA        VAL      K        5             5 ""       10.6 
#>  6 ATOM            38 CA        GLU      K        6             6 ""       10.8 
#>  7 ATOM            47 CA        SER      K        7             7 ""       12.6 
#>  8 ATOM            53 CA        GLY      K        8             8 ""       12.5 
#>  9 ATOM            57 CA        GLY      K        9             9 ""       14.3 
#> 10 ATOM            61 CA        GLY      K        10           10 ""       17.3 
#> # ℹ 242 more rows
#> # ℹ 4 more variables: y <dbl>, z <dbl>, aa1 <chr>, chain_type <chr>
